title: Singular function
content: In mathematics, a real-valued function "f" on the interval ["a", "b"] is said to be singular if it has the following properties: A standard example of a singular function is the Cantor function, which is sometimes called the devil's staircase (a term also used for singular functions in general). There are, however, other functions that have been given that name. One is defined in terms of the circle map.
